description_long: This lantern features a robust, geometric stone construction with a square base and a tapered central column. The upper section is adorned with a pagoda-style roof, complete with four upward-curving eaves and a spherical finial at the top. The lantern's midsection has four rectangular window-like openings, suggesting a space for a light source within. The surface is textured with a greenish-gray marbled stone pattern, giving it a natural and weathered appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional Japanese garden lanterns, often found in parks or tranquil urban spaces. Its solid build and ornamental roof make it both functional and decorative.
description: A stone lantern with a pagoda-style roof, marbled green-gray texture, and windowed midsection, reminiscent of traditional Japanese garden lanterns.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical, windowed midsection and the prominent pagoda roof with a spherical finial.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved eaves of the roof and the angular, tapered central column.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the lantern, similar to the front but with less visible window detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the stone texture and roof structure.
